title: Boots Riley Guesses Martin Scorsese Got ‘A Gang of Money’ for AI Partnership and ‘Doesn’t Give a F—‘
content:
ByPat Saperstein

Deputy Editor

Boots Rileycame out swinging afterMartin Scorseseannounced his involvement with the AI company Black Forest Labs on Tuesday, guessing that the filmmaker joined the firm as an advisor to help provide for his family and that Scorsese “doesn’t give a fuck” since he thinks AI will “fall on its face anyway.”

The “I Love Boosters” filmmaker posted on X: “My guess: at 83, they gave his family a gang of money (they throw tens of millions left&right) he wanted the income stream4them& feels like ‘AI’ will fall on its face anyway, so he doesnt give a fuck. If that’s not the case, extrafuck him. Separately, go see I Love Boosters today.”

Scorsese announced Tuesday that he wasjoining Black Forest Labsas an advisor to “push the bounds of creativity” and help create visuals such as storyboards.

Riley wrote that his issue with Scorsese’s support of the technology wasn’t as much about objecting to the 83-year-old filmmaker using AI in his work, but rather about using his influence to push others into using it.

“Like – yeah the problem with filmmaking is “we didn’t have the tools to be creative before this,” Rileywrote on X. “To b clear, my vitriol is not about him using it, I’d likely simply sneer at that in private. It’s about him using his cache 2 promote this and attempt to push the industry toward it. They need him. 1 Trillion spent on generative AI & it’s not saving anyone or changing film yet.”

“Cinema is a young medium, only around 125 years old, so we have to be open to how it can evolve,” Scorsese wrote on Black Forest’s website when his advisory role was announced. He said it would help him visualize more efficiently to his production designer, art designer and cinematographer.

Scorsese recently wrapped production on “What Happens At Night,” starring Leonardo DiCaprio and Jennifer Lawrence.

Riley has been active on social media promoting “I Love Boosters,” a comedy satirizing capitalism whic opened in theaters May 22 via Neon.
title_zh: 布茲·萊利(Boots Riley)猜測馬丁·史科西斯(Martin Scorsese)因為與人工智慧的合作「賺了一大筆錢」，而且「根本不在乎」。
content_zh:
由帕特·薩佩斯坦(Pat Saperstein)

副編輯

布茲·萊利(Boots Riley)在馬丁·斯科塞斯(Martin Scorsese)於週二宣布他參與人工智慧公司黑森林實驗室(Black Forest Labs)後，迅速發表了看法，猜測這位導演是作為顧問加入該公司的，以便為他的家庭提供支持，並表示斯科塞斯“根本不在乎”，因為他認為人工智慧“最終會失敗”。

這位《我愛助推器》(I Love Boosters)的導演在X平台上發文：“我的猜測是：在83歲時，他們給了他的家庭一大筆錢（他們隨便就花幾千萬），他想要為他們提供收入來源，並覺得‘人工智慧’最終會失敗，所以他根本不在乎。如果不是這樣，那就更糟了。另：今天去看《我愛助推器》。”

斯科塞斯於週二宣布他將加入黑森林實驗室作為顧問，旨在“推動創意的邊界”，並幫助創建視覺效果，例如故事板。

萊利表示，他對斯科塞斯支持這項技術的問題，不是反對這位83歲的導演在作品中使用人工智慧，而是關於他利用自己的影響力推動其他人使用它。

“就像——是的，電影製作的問題是‘在此之前我們沒有創造的工具’，”萊利在X上寫道。“要明確的是，我的憤怒並不是針對他使用它，我可能會私下對此嗤之以鼻。這是關於他利用自己的影響力來推廣這項技術，並試圖推動行業朝這個方向發展。他們需要他。1萬億花在生成式人工智慧上，但目前還沒有拯救任何人或改變電影。”

斯科塞斯在黑森林的網站上寫道：“電影是一種年輕的媒介，只有大約125年歷史，因此我們必須對它如何演變持開放態度。”他表示，這將幫助他更有效地向他的製作設計師、藝術設計師和攝影指導進行視覺化。

斯科塞斯最近完成了《夜間發生的事》(What Happens At Night)的拍攝，該片由李奧納多·迪卡普里奧(Leonardo DiCaprio)和詹妮弗·勞倫斯(Jennifer Lawrence)主演。

萊利在社交媒體上積極推廣《我愛助推器》，這是一部諷刺資本主義的喜劇，於5月22日通過Neon在影院上映。
summary_zh:
由帕特·薩佩斯坦(Pat Saperstein)

副編輯

布茲·萊利(Boots Riley)在馬丁·斯科塞斯(Martin Scorsese)於週二宣布他參與人工智慧公司黑森林實驗室(Black Forest Labs)後，迅速發表了看法，猜測這位導演是作為顧問加入該公司的，以便為他的家庭提供支持，並表示斯科塞斯“根本不在乎”，因為他認為人工智慧“最終會失敗”。
